title: [Bug]: PR label sync should report malformed GitHub JSON without a traceback
summary: The pull request label synchronization helper can surface raw JSON parsing failures if GitHub CLI returns malformed JSON or unexpected output.
observed: Malformed JSON from a GitHub CLI call can escape as a JSONDecodeError-style traceback instead of the helper's controlled error type.
expected: Malformed GitHub JSON should be reported as a PullRequestLabelSyncError with a clear human-readable message.
reproduction: Simulate gh returning non-JSON output for a JSON-capturing call in the label synchronization helper.
failing_test: Add a focused unit test for the JSON decoding helper or _run_gh capture path.
impact: Operators get noisier diagnostics during workflow failures. Runtime package behavior is unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This bug is limited to local GitHub workflow diagnostics and cannot affect sink writes or ACK behavior.
security: The error message should not echo large raw GitHub output, secrets, token values, private endpoints, or payload data.
acceptance: Malformed GitHub JSON is converted into PullRequestLabelSyncError.
The error message is concise and human-readable.
The message does not echo the raw malformed output.
A unit test covers the failure mode.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_pr_label_sync.py -q and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: No public documentation update is required.
closeout: Close after the bug branch merges into the release branch and release-gated closure confirms the release has shipped.
